title: Automate recurring email reminders of late tasks

posts.author: R2-Robert (Robert Tunick)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am looking on how to automate reminder emails of late tasks or other things.
I cannot use automations based on formulas (I wish I could).

Do you know to make this automation,

“When a DUE DATE is in the past and STATUS is not X, Y, or Z, send an email to OWNER, every Monday, Wednesday, Friday”

posts.author: WLane (Wally Lane)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @R2-Robert , You can use a template automation, not a custom automation to achieve this. Here’s an example of one that I have set up on a board. Every day at 6 AM, if the due date has passed (ensure the due date mode is enabled on the date column) and the status is not done, send a notification to the people column owner.

Note: this is an automation email though.

Hopefully that helps

image871×220 6.64 KB

image1810×816 109 KB

posts.author: R2-Robert (Robert Tunick)
posts.cookedHtml:
Wally,
Thank you so much for your reply and the work to put in a screenshot and details.
I am newer to Monday(dot)com and I had wrongly assumed “Notification” = only notify inside monday(dot)com. I see by default it sends an email, which would accomplish what I am trying to do.

This basically gets me to where I need to get. THANK YOU.
